title: hymenophyllum pectinatum
intro: Hymenophyllum pectinatum, also known as the forked spleenwort, is a type of fern that belongs to the family Hymenophyllaceae. It is native to Australia, New Zealand, and parts of Asia, and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In traditional herbal medicine, Hymenophyllum pectinatum is often prepared as a tea or infused in oil to utilize its medicinal properties.
